prequiz.title: Web Development Fundamentals
prequiz.subtitle: Key concepts to review before the quiz
prequiz.description: Familiarise yourself with these terms. A few Pokémon names are mixed in for fun — can you spot them?

keywords.definition: HyperText Markup Language — the standard language used to structure content on the web using elements and tags.
Cascading Style Sheets — a stylesheet language that controls the visual presentation of HTML elements.
A high-level, interpreted programming language that enables dynamic behaviour and interactivity in web pages.
Document Object Model — a programming interface that represents an HTML or XML document as a tree of nodes.
HyperText Transfer Protocol — the foundation of data communication on the web, used to transfer web resources.
Representational State Transfer API — an architectural style for building web services using standard HTTP methods.
JavaScript Object Notation — a lightweight, human-readable data-interchange format commonly used in web APIs.
A CSS layout model that allows items in a container to align and distribute space flexibly along a single axis.
A two-dimensional CSS layout system that arranges elements into rows and columns.
HTML tags that carry meaning about the content they contain, such as <header>, <article>, <nav>, and <footer>.
A web design approach that makes pages render well across a variety of devices and screen sizes.
The practice of designing and building web content that can be used by people with a wide range of abilities and disabilities.
JavaScript's mechanism for handling asynchronous operations by queuing callbacks and executing them one at a time.
A JavaScript object representing the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation.
Syntax that makes asynchronous, Promise-based code easier to read and write, behaving more like synchronous code.
